rxn_class_summary: Diastereodivergent bromoiminolactonization of α-allylmalonamides to bromo-substituted iminolactones, performed either electrochemically (with Zn(OTf)2/2,2'-bipyridine and Et4NBr) or chemically (NBS/Et4NBr).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (Et4NBr) generates an electrophilic brominating species (bromo cation / Br+ equivalent) that is delivered to the alkene; Et4N+ pairs with this bromo cation to modulate selectivity.

key_intermediates.name: electrochemically generated bromo cation (Br+ equivalent) complexed with Et4N+
bromonium ion on allyl alkene of α-allylmalonamide

electron_flow: Bromide is oxidized at the anode (directly or via Et4NBr) to an electrophilic bromo cation that forms a bromonium ion on the allyl group; intramolecular 5-exo attack by the amide oxygen closes the iminolactone with preferential trans diastereoselectivity.

probe_reagents.name: NBS / Et4NBr (chemical control)
probe_reagents.observation: Chemical bromocyclization with NBS/Et4NBr also gave trans-2a' preferentially but with lower dr than the electrochemical conditions, supporting that an Et4NBr–bromo cation complex (rather than Zn(OTf)2/bipy alone) governs selectivity.

probe_reagents.name: oxalic acid hydrolysis of 2a/2a'
probe_reagents.observation: Iminolactones 2a/2a' hydrolyzed cleanly to the corresponding lactones 3a (82%) and 3a' (85%) with retention of stereochemistry.

probe_reagents.name: 4-bromobenzenethiol substitution
probe_reagents.observation: Nucleophilic displacement of the C–Br in 2a/2a' afforded thioether iminolactones 4a (91%) and 4a' (92%), confirming the bromide position and configuration.

notes: The authors explicitly state that the role of Zn(OTf)2 and 2,2'-bipyridine in the electrochemical reaction remains unclear; they propose that Et4NBr complexes with an anodically generated bromo cation to control diastereoselectivity. No radical traps, CV, or DFT studies are reported on these pages; mechanistic support is indirect (comparison of chemical vs electrochemical bromination and product derivatization). Asymmetric variants and deeper mechanistic studies are stated to be ongoing.
